citizenUpdates.narrative: Jasmine's Route 47 rerouted via Mission Street, adding 35 minutes each way.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 1: Route diverted through Mission — 35 extra minutes each way. Scrambling for after-school coverage.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tony's restaurant entrance partially blocked by construction hoarding. Lunch crowd down 30%.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 1: Hoarding reduces storefront visibility. Revenue down ~30%.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia watches the excavation crews from the sidewalk, hoping her contract inspector position starts soon.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 1: Contract not yet active. Waiting on SF Public Works onboarding.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl's walking route to CPMC Van Ness is now blocked by construction barriers. Taking a longer detour.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 1: CPMC route blocked. Relying on neighbors for rides.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Amy's school not yet affected — Block A excavation is 400m south of Tenderloin Community School.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 1: Not yet affected. Keeping a dust log as precaution.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler's protected bike lane on Van Ness is gone. He's riding in traffic, nervous but managing.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 1: Bike lane removed. Riding in mixed traffic. Feels unsafe.

events.title: Block A Excavation Commences
events.description: Bay Area Infrastructure Inc. begins excavation on the 0.8km Block A stretch of Van Ness Avenue. 3 excavators deployed. Southbound lanes closed to vehicles from 7am-6pm.

events.title: Van Ness Bus Routes Rerouted
events.description: SFMTA Muni routes 47 and 49 rerouted via Mission Street. Journey time increase: 30-40 minutes per trip. 22,000 daily riders affected.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Jasmine adjusts to the longer route. Her neighbor Maria picks up the kids on Tuesdays and Thursdays.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 2: Route adds 35 min. Neighbor helping with pickup twice a week.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tony puts a chalk sandwich board on the sidewalk directing customers around the hoarding. Lunch crowd still down 40%.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 2: Sandwich board workaround but lunch crowd still down 40%.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia gets the call from SF Public Works — she starts as Contract Inspector on Monday. First major role since graduation.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 2: EMPLOYED — Contract Inspector, SF DPW. $7,400/month.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl's nephew offers him a Lyft account. Earl refuses — says he'll wait for the bus shelter to reopen.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 2: Nephew offered Lyft account. Refused. Waiting for bus access to normalize.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Amy notices dust levels slightly elevated but classrooms are manageable with windows on the north side.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 2: Slight dust increase. Using north-facing windows as precaution.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler has a near-miss with a delivery truck on Van Ness. Files a cycling safety complaint with SFMTA.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 2: Near-miss incident with delivery truck. SFMTA complaint filed. Shaken.

events.title: Sofia Rodriguez Assigned — Road Inspector
events.description: SF Public Works assigns Sofia Rodriguez (C003) as contract quality control inspector for Block A and B. First major professional role since Caltrain Engineering graduation.

events.title: Block A Progress — 40%
events.description: Excavation at Block A reaches 40% completion. Utility undergrounding by DPW General Contractors LLC begins.

label: Month 3 — Atmospheric River Delays
weatherEvent: Atmospheric river event — 14 workdays lost across Block A and B. Contractor B flagged for not deploying additional crew to recover schedule.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Rain plus the Mission Street diversion adds another 20 minutes on bad days. Jasmine is exhausted.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 3: Atmospheric river + diversion = 55 extra minutes on bad days. Running on empty.

citizenUpdates.narrative: The heavy rain keeps customers home anyway. Tony uses the quiet period to repaint the dining room.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 3: Rain suppressed footfall. Using downtime to refurbish interior.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia documents the rain damage to freshly-dug trenches. Files a delay report with SF Public Works.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 3: Filed delay report. Trench flooding documented. Building reputation for diligence.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl stays indoors during the rain. His neighbor DeShawn makes a Walgreens run for his insulin supplies.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 3: Housebound during rain event. Neighbor doing pharmacy runs.

citizenUpdates.narrative: The rain suppresses construction dust. Amy opens the classroom windows for the first time since construction started.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 3: Rain suppresses dust. Brief reprieve for the classroom.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler works from home during the storms. No cycling. He starts browsing Oakland co-working spaces online.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 3: WFH during storms. Casually browsing Oakland listings.

events.title: Contractor B — Atmospheric River Delay Flag
events.description: Bay Area Infrastructure Inc. (Block B) loses 14 workdays to atmospheric river but does not deploy additional crew to recover schedule. Delay: 21 days projected.

events.title: Weather Impact — 14 Workdays Lost
events.description: Atmospheric river event halts all excavation activity on Van Ness Avenue. Open trenches require emergency shoring. SFPUC emergency protocols activated.

label: Month 4 — Block A Complete, Block B Begins

citizenUpdates.narrative: Block A section reopens — Jasmine's Route 47 slightly improved, but Block B closure creates a new bottleneck near City Hall.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 4: Block A open. Small relief. Block B now the bottleneck.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Block B construction moves directly in front of Ricci's. Entrance completely hoarded. Revenue down 60%.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 4: CRISIS — Revenue -60%. Entrance hoarded. Cannot cover payroll.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia's inspection report leads to a quality checkpoint being added for Block B concrete spec. She is mentioned in the site bulletin.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 4: Quality checkpoint added from her report. Getting recognition.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl counts the months on his calendar. 8 more to go. He marks each day off like a countdown.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 4: Calendar countdown. 8 months remaining. Isolation deepening.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Block B start means Amy's school is now in the construction corridor. She begins keeping a daily dust and noise log.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 4: School now in construction corridor. Daily monitoring started.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler attends a District 6 community meeting and presents his cycling lane petition. Gets 85 signatures from Hayes Valley neighbors.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 4: Petition presented at D6 meeting. SFMTA said they will 'review'.

events.title: Block A Excavation — Complete
events.description: Block A (0.8km) excavation complete on schedule. BRT lane sub-base preparation begins next month.

events.title: Block B — Van Ness Businesses Directly Affected
events.description: Construction moves to Block B. Restaurant and retail frontages directly blocked including Ricci's Italian Kitchen (C002). Peak disruption phase begins.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Jasmine starts an informal carpool with two other Muni drivers to share the longer Mission Street route.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 5: Carpool formed with two colleagues. Sharing the burden. Still 35 min extra.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tony's wife suggests closing for the summer and visiting family in Genoa. Tony refuses — says he owes it to his father.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 5: Wife wants to close and travel. Tony refuses. Business loan taken to cover staff wages.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia raises the PM2.5 issue at the site safety meeting. Dust screens installed within 48 hours.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 5: Dust screens installed after her report. Respect growing on site.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl's diabetes reading is elevated — doctor says stress and reduced walking are contributing. Recommends vitamin D.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 5: A1C elevated. Doctor concerned about inactivity and isolation.

citizenUpdates.narrative: One of Amy's asthmatic students has a breathing episode during recess. She files a formal PM2.5 complaint with SF DPW noise hotline.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 5: FORMAL COMPLAINT FILED. Asthmatic student needed inhaler during recess.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler's SFMTA petition gets a response — agency says cycling infrastructure will be 'considered in Phase 2'. Tyler posts the reply on Twitter.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 5: SFMTA says cycling lanes will be 'Phase 2'. Cautiously furious.

events.title: School Dust Complaint — Amy Park
events.description: Amy Park (C005), teacher at Tenderloin Community School, files formal PM2.5 complaint with SF DPW. School is 200m from active Block B construction.

events.title: BRT Lane Sub-base — Block A Begins
events.description: Class C5 concrete BRT lane sub-base work begins on completed Block A. Bay Area Infrastructure Inc. deploys 2 paving machines.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Jasmine calculates she has driven 1,400 extra kilometres in 6 months. Her bus is due for a 50k mile service.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 6: 1,400 extra km driven. Vehicle maintenance due early. Union grievance filed.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tony withdraws $18,000 from his savings to cover staff wages. His daughter starts working weekends for free.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 6: $18K from savings withdrawn. Daughter helping on weekends. Shop barely surviving.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia is asked to formally document the contractor delay. She does so methodically — her first major professional milestone.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 6: Formal delay documentation completed. First major professional milestone.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl's neighbor DeShawn has started driving him to CPMC every two weeks. Earl brings him a coffee and a newspaper.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 6: CPMC access via neighbor established. Community support holding him up.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Dust screens have helped somewhat. Amy keeps windows closed on the south side. Students are restless in the heat.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 6: Dust screens installed. South windows still closed. Students restless.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler hires his second full-time employee. They celebrate by cycling to Mission Dolores, the long way round.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 6: Second hire. Celebrating by cycling to the Mission. Still no Van Ness lane.

events.title: RED FLAG — Contractor B Milestone Missed
events.description: Bay Area Infrastructure Inc. misses Month 6 milestone on Block B excavation. Delay: 21 days beyond projected. Red flag raised. SF Public Works penalty clause review initiated.

events.title: Midpoint — 50% Construction Complete
events.description: Van Ness Complete Streets project reaches 50% completion. Block A BRT paving complete. Block B excavation 3 weeks delayed.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Jasmine is invited to testify at the City Hall midpoint review. She speaks for 3 minutes and the Supervisor thanks her by name.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 7: Testified at City Hall review. Her experience heard officially for the first time.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tony addresses the midpoint review via public comment and demands immediate business disruption checks. Told to resubmit his OEWD application.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 7: Demanded compensation at midpoint review. Told to resubmit application. Furious.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia's delay documentation is cited in the official midpoint report. Her project manager recommends her for a permanent DPW role.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 7: Work cited in official SF Public Works midpoint report. Permanent role recommendation submitted.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl watches the City Hall review livestream on his phone. A supervisor mentions 'seniors displaced from medical services on Van Ness'. He feels seen.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 7: Supervisor mentioned senior medical access. Watched on livestream. Feels acknowledged.

citizenUpdates.narrative: The midpoint review commits to PM2.5 monitoring and construction halt protocol from Month 8. Amy is cautiously optimistic.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 7: PM2.5 monitoring committed from Month 8. Waiting to see if it's enforced.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler submits a detailed protected cycling infrastructure proposal to the District 6 Supervisor. Gets a 10-minute meeting scheduled.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 7: Cycling proposal submitted to D6 Supervisor. Meeting scheduled. Cautiously hopeful.

events.title: Midpoint City Hall Review Conducted
events.description: Official midpoint review of Van Ness Complete Streets project held at SF City Hall. Citizens testify. Jasmine Chen and Tony Ricci address Supervisor. Contractor penalty clause discussion initiated.

events.title: Sofia Rodriguez — Permanent DPW Role Recommended
events.description: Sofia's midpoint report cited by SF Public Works project director. Formal recommendation for permanent civil engineering position submitted to HR.

citizenUpdates.narrative: A second section of Van Ness reopens. Jasmine's route improves by 12 minutes. She can see the end.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 8: Route 12 min shorter than peak disruption. Down to 23 extra min. Getting there.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Block B moves past Ricci's. Tony unbolts the hoarding access panel and puts a welcome mat out. His regulars are trickling back.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 8: Hoarding removed from entrance. Welcome mat out. Revenue at 55%.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia oversees the Block B concrete handover inspection. She catches 2 compaction issues — both corrected before sign-off.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 8: Block B signed off after 2 compaction corrections. Professional reputation solidifying.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl marks Month 8 on his calendar. 4 more months. He tells DeShawn he can see the light at the end of the tunnel.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 8: 4 months left. Telling neighbors he can see the end coming.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Block B moves north of the school. Dust levels drop. Amy opens the classroom windows for the first time in months.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 8: Construction moved north of school. Windows open. Students breathing easier.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler hears that Block C design includes a protected cycling track. He shares the update on LinkedIn — 600 reactions.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 8: Block C to include protected cycling track. Sharing the news. Daring to hope.

events.title: Block B Excavation — Complete (21 Days Late)
events.description: Block B excavation complete, 21 days behind schedule due to atmospheric river + contractor delay. SF Public Works penalty clause invoked: $280,000 deducted from Bay Area Infrastructure Inc. final payment.

events.title: Block C Excavation Begins — Golden Gate Civil Works
events.description: Golden Gate Civil Works begins Block C (0.7km) excavation on Van Ness. On schedule. No flags.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Jasmine can see Block C progress from her bus window. She calculates 3 more months and starts to believe it.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 9: Can see Block C progress from bus. 3 months left. Cautiously hopeful.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Revenue back to 60%. Tony hires back his part-time server who left in Month 4. First new payroll in 5 months.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 9: Revenue at 60%. Part-time server rehired. Cautious optimism.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia receives her permanent DPW offer letter. She accepts on the spot. Her mom takes her out for dinner in the Mission.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 9: PERMANENT OFFER ACCEPTED. $8,200/month SF DPW role. Career relaunched.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl's doctor clears him for short walks. He does laps in his building hallway, waiting for the Van Ness sidewalk.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 9: Cleared for short walks. Doing hallway laps. Waiting for the sidewalk.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Amy's school files a formal request to be included in the Van Ness corridor community benefit commitments.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 9: Community benefit request filed. Asking for air filtration system funded from project budget.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler has a second near-miss — construction truck runs a red on Van Ness. That evening he tours an Oakland office space.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 9: Second near-miss. Touring Oakland co-working spaces. Decision nearly made.

events.title: Tyler Brooks — Relocation Intent Filed
events.description: Tyler Brooks (C006), startup founder and daily cyclist, tours Oakland co-working space after second near-miss incident on Van Ness. Cites removal of cycling infrastructure as primary driver.

events.title: Sofia Rodriguez — Permanent DPW Appointment
events.description: Sofia Rodriguez receives and accepts permanent civil engineering position with SF Public Works. Salary: $8,200/month. Start date: Month 10.

citizenUpdates.narrative: The pipe burst causes another week of detours. Jasmine texts her SFMTA union rep about overtime pay.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 10: Another week of detours from pipe burst. Union grievance filed for overtime compensation.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tony sees the pipe burst on Nextdoor and grips his coffee mug. He cannot absorb another month of reduced revenue.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 10: Pipe burst news worrying. Cannot handle another delay. Watching the ledger daily.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia is called in from her new permanent role to assess the pipe burst damage. Her expertise is now formally recognized across departments.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 10: Called in to assess pipe burst. Expertise recognized across DPW divisions.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl hears about the pipe burst on KQED. He shakes his head and says this city never changes. Then he makes some tea.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 10: Not directly affected. Philosophical about it. Listening to KQED.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Amy hears about the $4M budget addition and immediately emails the principal about redirecting funds for school air filtration.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 10: Demanding $150K of pipe burst supplementary budget for school air filtration system.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler signs his Oakland lease. He tells his startup team they move to a Jack London Square co-working space from Month 11.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 10: Oakland lease signed. Team notified of move. This is final.

events.title: Unplanned Utility Pipe Burst — $4M Budget Addition
events.description: SFPUC water main discovered during Block C excavation. Emergency repair required. Budget impact: +$4,000,000. Timeline impact: +21 days. SF Controller watchdog flag raised.

events.title: Block C Progress — 60%
events.description: Block C excavation at 60% despite pipe burst setback. Golden Gate Civil Works working extended hours to recover schedule.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Jasmine can see the new BRT lane surface on Block A and B from her bus. Almost done. She dares to be hopeful.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 11: Almost done. Route still 25 min extra but hope has returned.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tony's revenue is back to 85%. He orders new outdoor planters for the sidewalk. The regulars are coming back. He doesn't tell Maria about the savings.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 11: Revenue at 85%. New outdoor planters ordered. Keeping the loan secret from Maria.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia is now coordinating 3 junior inspectors on site. Her permanent DPW manager calls her the best hire of the year.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 11: Coordinating 3 junior inspectors. Called best DPW hire of the year.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl can see construction winding down from his window. He calls his nephew in Sacramento for the first time in months.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 11: Watching construction wind down from window. Called family. Things looking up.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Construction dust is nearly gone. Amy reopens all classroom windows. Her asthmatic student's peak flow has improved.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 11: All windows open. Asthmatic student's readings improved. Relief throughout the school.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler is fully settled in Oakland. He follows the Van Ness completion news on LinkedIn. He writes a thread: 'I left SF because they forgot cyclists.'
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 11: IN OAKLAND. Following project news on LinkedIn. Writing thread about why he left SF.

events.title: Block C Excavation — Complete
events.description: Block C (0.7km) excavation complete. Golden Gate Civil Works finished 4 days ahead of revised schedule. BRT lane paving begins.

events.title: Final BRT Paving — All Blocks Active
events.description: Simultaneous BRT lane paving on Block B remainder and Block C. Golden Gate Civil Works begins signage and road markings installation.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Jasmine's Route 47 is fully restored on Van Ness. Her trip is 8 minutes shorter than before construction. Home by 5:15.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 12: Route restored. 8 min shorter than before. Home for the kids every night.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Ricci's Italian Kitchen has its best Friday night in 14 months. Tony's daughter frames the menu from his father's opening night. They survived.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 12: Best Friday in 14 months. Framed original menu on the wall. Survived.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Sofia walks the entire 2.3km at the ribbon-cutting ceremony. She supervised every meter of it.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 12: Walked the full road at ribbon-cutting. Her road. Her win.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Earl Washington walks to CPMC Van Ness Medical Center for the first time in 12 months. The new sidewalk has a railing. He takes it slowly, and smiles.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 12: WALKED TO CPMC. First time in 12 months. New sidewalk with railing. Doctor says he looks 10 years younger.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Amy's class plants 4 new street trees at the school block as part of the 3:1 replacement program. The kids name one of them 'Biscuit'.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 12: Trees planted. Students healing the neighborhood. AQI back to baseline.

citizenUpdates.narrative: Tyler watches the Van Ness ribbon-cutting livestream from his Oakland office. He tweets: 'Glad it is done. Still no protected bike lane.' He does not come back.
citizenUpdates.newPolicyImpact: Month 12: IN OAKLAND. Watching livestream remotely. No protected bike lane installed. Won't return.

events.title: VAN NESS COMPLETE STREETS — PHASE 1 DONE
events.description: 2.3km Van Ness Avenue Complete Streets redesign officially complete. 21 days behind schedule. Final cost: $49.2M ($4.2M over budget due to pipe burst). BRT lanes, cycle tracks, and new bus shelters open to the public.

events.title: Accountability Ledger Final — Contractor B Penalised
events.description: Bay Area Infrastructure Inc.: $280,000 penalty deducted for 21-day delay. DPW General Contractors LLC: formal warning issued. Golden Gate Civil Works: commendation letter issued.
